gloss: whoa!, oops! (surprise or minor mishap)
sent1: Eita, eu esqueci o guarda-chuva de novo.
sent1_en: Whoa, I forgot the umbrella again.
sent2: Eita, caiu tudo no chão!
sent2_en: Whoa, everything fell on the floor!
notes: Interjection for sudden surprise or a small mishap; extremely common in spoken BR.

gloss: huh, well (Minas Gerais interjection of surprise/obviousness)
sent1: Uai, você não sabia disso?
sent1_en: Huh, you didn't know that?
sent2: Uai, mas é claro que eu vou!
sent2_en: Well, of course I'm going!
notes: Regional interjection strongly associated with Minas Gerais; marks surprise or stating the obvious.
ear: one glide, not three sounds: uai -> [uaj]

gloss: whoa!, oops! (surprise or abrupt greeting)
sent1: Opa, cuidado com o degrau aí!
sent1_en: Whoa, watch out for that step!
sent2: Opa, tudo bem contigo?
sent2_en: Hey, how's it going?
notes: Casual interjection: surprise, apologizing for bumping someone, or a brusque hello.

gloss: alright, deal, see ya (closing a conversation/agreement)
sent1: Falou, te vejo amanhã então.
sent1_en: Alright, see you tomorrow then.
sent2: Falou, pode deixar que eu cuido disso.
sent2_en: Deal, I'll take care of that.
notes: Closes a conversation or confirms agreement; unrelated to its literal meaning 'spoke'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es '(él/ella) habló' aquí. Es una muletilla de cierre, como 'vale' o 'hecho'.

gloss: get it?, got it? (slang comprehension check)
sent1: Você tem que apertar aqui primeiro, sacou?
sent1_en: You have to press here first, get it?
sent2: Ela sacou na hora que eu tava brincando.
sent2_en: She got it right away that I was joking.
notes: Slang for 'to understand', from 'sacar' (to pull out); means 'get it' here, not withdrawing money.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'sacar dinero' aquí. Es jerga para 'entender, pillar'.

gloss: damn, ugh (euphemism for annoyance/disappointment)
sent1: Putz, eu esqueci a senha de novo.
sent1_en: Ugh, I forgot the password again.
sent2: Putz, hoje o trânsito tá um caos.
sent2_en: Damn, traffic is chaos today.
notes: Mild euphemistic interjection of frustration, softer than a real curse word.

gloss: huh?, what? (interjection of puzzlement/surprise)
sent1: Ué, cadê o resto do bolo?
sent1_en: Huh, where's the rest of the cake?
sent2: Ué, você não ia sair hoje?
sent2_en: Huh, weren't you going out today?
notes: Expresses mild confusion or surprise, often when something doesn't match expectations.

gloss: oh okay, oh I see (sudden realization/acknowledgment)
sent1: Ah tá, agora eu entendi o que você quis dizer.
sent1_en: Oh I see, now I understand what you meant.
sent2: Ah tá, então foi por isso que ele não veio.
sent2_en: Oh okay, so that's why he didn't come.
notes: Signals sudden understanding or acknowledgment of new information.

gloss: huh?, what? (asking for repetition or seeking confirmation)
sent1: Hein? Pode repetir, eu não entendi.
sent1_en: Huh? Can you repeat that, I didn't understand.
sent2: Você vai mesmo, hein?
sent2_en: You're really going, huh?
notes: Filler for asking someone to repeat, or a sentence-final tag seeking confirmation.

gloss: okay?, you hear? (sentence-final tag for emphasis/confirmation)
sent1: Chega cedo amanhã, viu?
sent1_en: Get here early tomorrow, okay?
sent2: Não esquece de me ligar, viu?
sent2_en: Don't forget to call me, you hear?
notes: Sentence-final tag for emphasis or seeking agreement, unrelated to the verb 'ver' (to see).
cuidado: ⚠️ No es '(tú) viste' aquí. Es una coletilla de énfasis al final de la frase.

gloss: okay, alright; how's it going? (greeting/agreement)
sent1: Tudo bem, a gente resolve isso depois.
sent1_en: Alright, we'll sort that out later.
sent2: Oi, tudo bem? Faz tempo que a gente não se vê.
sent2_en: Hey, how's it going? It's been a while since we've seen each other.
notes: Functions both as a greeting question and as an agreement/acceptance phrase.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es solo 'todo bien' como afirmación; también funciona como saludo, '¿qué tal?'.

gloss: it's just that, the thing is (introduces a justification)
sent1: Eu não fui, é que eu tava muito cansado.
sent1_en: I didn't go, it's just that I was really tired.
sent2: Ela chegou atrasada, é que o ônibus quebrou.
sent2_en: She arrived late, the thing is the bus broke down.
notes: Introduces an explanation or excuse, softer than a bare 'porque'.

gloss: in that case, actually, specifically (clarifying qualifier)
sent1: No caso, eu prefiro ir de manhã.
sent1_en: In that case, I'd rather go in the morning.
sent2: No caso, foi ela quem esqueceu a chave.
sent2_en: Actually, it was she who forgot the key.
notes: Used to specify or clarify a previous statement, similar to 'actually' or 'specifically'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No siempre equivale a 'en el caso de'; suele usarse para precisar o matizar lo dicho.

gloss: I mean, that is to say (reformulator)
sent1: Ele vem amanhã, quer dizer, depois de amanhã.
sent1_en: He's coming tomorrow, I mean, the day after tomorrow.
sent2: A gente vai atrasar, quer dizer, se o trânsito continuar assim.
sent2_en: We're going to be late, that is, if traffic keeps up like this.
notes: Reformulates or corrects what was just said, mid-sentence.

gloss: that is, in other words (reformulator)
sent1: Ele chegou depois das dez, ou seja, bem atrasado.
sent1_en: He arrived after ten, in other words, quite late.
sent2: Ninguém confirmou, ou seja, ninguém vai vir.
sent2_en: Nobody confirmed, that is, nobody's coming.
notes: Introduces a restatement or clarifying conclusion.

gloss: like, kind of (vague filler, emphasized)
sent1: Eu fiquei, tipo assim, sem saber o que falar.
sent1_en: I was, like, at a loss for words.
sent2: A festa foi, tipo assim, meio estranha.
sent2_en: The party was, kind of, a bit weird.
notes: Filler expressing vagueness or hesitation, common in casual youth speech.

gloss: what's up?, so then (greeting or topic transition)
sent1: E aí, beleza? Faz tempo que a gente não se fala.
sent1_en: What's up, all good? It's been a while since we talked.
sent2: E aí, o que você decidiu sobre a viagem?
sent2_en: So, what did you decide about the trip?
notes: Common casual greeting or discourse marker to pick up a topic.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'y allí'; es un saludo casual o un conector para retomar el tema.

gloss: chill, no worries (informal calm acceptance)
sent1: Pode deixar o prato aí, de boa.
sent1_en: You can leave the plate there, no worries.
sent2: Ele ficou de boa mesmo perdendo o jogo.
sent2_en: He stayed chill even after losing the game.
notes: Informal expression for being relaxed or okay with something.

gloss: oh my God, good grief (surprise/exasperation)
sent1: Meu Deus, que susto você me deu!
sent1_en: Oh my God, you scared me!
sent2: Meu Deus, olha que trânsito é esse.
sent2_en: Good grief, look at this traffic.
notes: Common interjection of surprise, shock, or exasperation.

gloss: uh-oh, oh no (Northeastern interjection of alarm/surprise)
sent1: Vixe, acho que eu deixei o forno ligado.
sent1_en: Uh-oh, I think I left the oven on.
sent2: Vixe, ela caiu com tudo ali.
sent2_en: Oh no, she fell hard right there.
notes: Interjection of alarm or surprise, originally Northeastern Brazilian, now widely used.
